text: William James, writing in 1890, observed that "the faculty of voluntarily bringing back a wandering attention, over and over again, is the very root of judgment, character, and will." He recognized what modern neuroimaging would later confirm: sustained attention is not passive but an active, effortful process requiring continual re-engagement. The default mode network, the brain system active during mind-wandering, does not switch off automatically when a task demands focus — it must be actively suppressed by the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex. Digital environments that reward novelty-seeking actively strengthen mind-wandering pathways at the expense of this suppression mechanism.
question: What must the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex do to enable focused attention?

options.text: Activate the default mode network to generate task-relevant associations
Increase glucose supply to sensory processing regions
Actively suppress the default mode network's mind-wandering activity
Synchronize firing patterns with the hippocampus to consolidate task rules
